user_outcome: sworn reads a real coach-produced board.json whose `release` is the canonical baton OBJECT form ({name, vertical_trace, ...}) without an unmarshal error, so the oracle and `sworn run` can load a real release instead of failing at board-read — the board-level companion to D6.
rationale: Same bug as D6, one level up: sworn's Go BoardRecord.Release is `string`, but the canonical baton board-v1 (~/projects/baton/schemas/board-v1.json) defines `release` as an OBJECT with required `name` (and vertical_trace — the Rule-8 golden thread). sworn's embedded board-v1 + Go struct are pinned to an older baton (the binary reports Baton v0.6.3) and lag the contract. Every coach-produced fired board uses the canonical object form, so the installed oracle reads NONE of them: `sworn board --release 2026-06-28-yearSnapshot-schema-cleanup` fails with 'cannot unmarshal object into BoardRecord.release of type string'. Direction is settled by the canonical baton schema: migrate sworn UP to the object form, do NOT downgrade boards to string (an earlier downgrade of THIS release's own board.json was wrong-direction and is reverted once this lands). Fix is surgical: make BoardRecord.Release tolerate both the canonical object and the legacy string; reconcile the embedded board-v1 schema; update the one value-consumer (cmd/sworn/board.go). The full baton re-vendor (all schemas) is a larger follow-up; this slice is the board-read unblock only.

effort_complexity.rationale: A custom UnmarshalJSON on the release field (accept string OR {name}), one value-consumer update (cmd/sworn/board.go:110), an embedded-schema reconcile, and tests. Well-bounded; oracle.go (T1) does not read .Release, so disjoint and low-risk.

acceptance_criteria.text: When the oracle reads a board.json whose `release` is a JSON object {"name": "<release>", ...} (canonical baton board-v1, e.g. fired's coach-produced boards), ReadBoard / readTrackInfos SHALL unmarshal it without error and resolve the release name from release.name.
When the oracle reads a board.json whose `release` is a bare JSON string (legacy form), it SHALL still unmarshal successfully, so existing string-form boards keep working (back-compatible).

BoardRecord.Release SHALL be a Go type that unmarshals from BOTH a JSON string and a JSON object with a `name` field, exposing the release name to callers; the sole value-consumer (cmd/sworn/board.go ~line 110, `bs.Release`) SHALL be updated to read the name. A missing/empty name on the object form SHALL be an unmarshal error (fail closed).

The embedded internal/baton/schemas/board-v1.json SHALL be reconciled toward the canonical baton shape (release as object with required name) so embedded schema validation accepts real coach boards; the Go reader SHALL remain tolerant of the legacy string form regardless. Migrate UP to canonical — never downgrade the schema to string.

When `sworn board --release 2026-06-28-yearSnapshot-schema-cleanup` is run against the live fired repo (--docs-prefix apps/docs/content/docs) — the board that today fails with 'cannot unmarshal object into BoardRecord.release of type string' — it SHALL print the board (track T1-schema-cleanup + its slices) with no parse error. Reachability artefact: capture that output.
After the change, `go build ./...` SHALL succeed and `go test ./internal/board/...` SHALL pass (including new tests for both release shapes).

If sworn writes the board back (WriteBoard / materialise), the round-trip SHALL preserve every field of the canonical `release` object (name, vertical_trace, target_version, integration_branch) — no field dropped — exactly as the D6 deferral round-trip requires. A read->write fixture test SHALL prove vertical_trace survives. (Achievable by preserving the raw object on the Release type and re-emitting it; a legacy string round-trips as a string.)
